item.title: Gen-Z Gamer’s 3D-Model Startup Becomes China’s Latest AI Unicorn
item.description: Vast, a 3D-modeling startup founded by a 29-year-old gamer, raised nearly $200 million to become the latest Chinese AI outfit to achieve a valuation of $1 billion.

item.title: Powell Says Fed Credibility Lost If President Can Fire Officials
item.description: Former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said the US central bank would lose the credibility that’s required to support a strong and stable economy if any president were free to dismiss Fed officials over policy disagreements.

item.title: China Investors Turn Sellers of HK Stocks in Rare Reversal
item.description: Chinese mainland investors turned net sellers of Hong Kong stocks for the first time in nearly three years in May, after a surge in tech shares trading onshore eroded the appeal of the city’s market.

item.title: Best ECB Response to Stablecoins Is Digital Euro, Schnabel Says
item.description: Stablecoins pose a number of risks to financial stability and monetary policy, and the European Central Bank’s best response is to ensure that public money remains the anchor of the system, according to Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

item.title: Japan’s Firms Cut Spending Over Iran Stress Even as Profits Firm
item.description: Japan’s biggest companies reduced capital spending in the first quarter even as ordinary profits at manufacturers reached a record, as emerging uncertainty from escalating turbulence in the Middle East clouded prospects for future growth.

item.title: Backrooms, Obsession Usher in the Era of the YouTube Filmmaker
item.description: Low-budget horror movies directed by YouTube creators are proving more popular than Star Wars

item.title: Vietnam’s Gene Solutions Eyes Hong Kong IPO to Fund Expansion
item.description: Vietnamese diagnostic testing startup Gene Solutions is preparing for a Hong Kong initial public offering as early as the second quarter of next year, a rare local firm seeking to access deeper capital markets and boost international visibility.

item.title: South Korea’s Export Momentum Continues, Backing BOK’s Hawkish Case
item.description: South Korea’s exports extended their strong run in May, reinforcing the central bank’s increasingly hawkish stance as booming semiconductor demand continues to underpin growth despite rising geopolitical and inflation risks.

item.title: TSMC’s Local Investors Narrow Valuation Gap With Wall Street
item.description: Local investors are driving the premium on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.’s US-listed shares to a two-year low as they pile into the chipmaker’s stock in Taipei on bets the AI boom has further to run.

item.title: Dell Rivals Apple’s MacBook Neo With $699 Touch-Screen XPS 13 Laptop
item.description: Dell Technologies Inc. introduced a redesigned XPS 13 laptop, positioning the device as a direct answer to Apple Inc.’s popular MacBook Neo, which stunned the budget PC market upon its debut in March with its features and price.

item.title: China’s Shoppers Are Buying Luxury Again as Stock Market Rebounds
item.description: Chinese consumers are showing signs of a renewed appetite for high-end beauty and fashion products, a rare bright spot for global luxury brands after years of weak demand and margin-eroding discounts in their most important growth market.

item.title: Oil Climbs as US-Iran Ceasefire Remains Elusive: Markets Wrap
item.description: Oil climbed as tensions remained elevated in the Middle East, with negotiations over a permanent US-Iran ceasefire showing little sign of a breakthrough.

item.title: Oil Rises From Six-Week Low Amid Uncertainty Over US-Iran Deal
item.description: Oil rose from a six-week low amid uncertainty over the outlook for a peace deal to end the war in Iran.

item.title: Petrobras Cuts Diesel Prices Amid Federal Subsidy Plan
item.description: Brazil’s state-controlled oil giant Petroleo Brasileiro SA is reducing domestic diesel prices starting Monday as part of a government subsidy program to shield consumers from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: SpaceX’s IPO Forces Wall Street to Reorganize Around It
item.description: The company’s ambitious listing plan is set to clear the way for other mega-offerings. It also risks threatening the integrity of the market itself

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway to Buy Taylor Morrison for $6.8 Billion
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. will acquire Taylor Morrison Home Corp. in an all-cash deal worth about $6.8 billion, the two companies announced on Sunday.

item.title: Mexico’s Fibra MTY to Buy Fibra Macquarie in $1.7 Billion Deal
item.description: Mexican real estate investment trust Fibra MTY said it’s acquiring peer Fibra Macquarie after the latter’s shareholders backed the transaction.

item.title: Wall Street Risk Rally Powers Past War Stalemate
item.description: Three months into the Iran war, it has become all but impossible to damp the bullish spirit driving global markets for risky assets.

item.title: Bond Trader Bets on Fed Hike Poised for Gut Check From Jobs Data
item.description: Bond traders are looking to a key jobs report this week to confirm their wagers that the US economy is strong enough to push the Federal Reserve to lift interest rates by next year.

item.title: Indian Exam Board Admits to Cybersecurity Flaws Found by Teen
item.description: A national school exam board in India said it has been monitoring and has contained vulnerabilities in its online grading portal for one of the country’s most important school-leaving exams that were first flagged by a teenage cybersecurity researcher.

item.title: Australia Home Prices Flatline as Rate Hikes, Tax Changes Weigh
item.description: Australian home prices stagnated last month, with growth weakening across most major cities as higher borrowing costs and tax reforms sapped demand.

item.title: Apple Seeks to Disrupt the Glasses Market the Way It Did With Watches
item.description: Also: The latest on iOS 27, iOS 28, new Apple TV and HomePod mini.

item.title: Big Tech Is Suddenly Impacting Niche Market for US Dividends
item.description: The dominance of a few technology giants in the benchmark S&P 500 Index is now carrying over into the niche market for dividend futures and options.

item.title: Fed’s Waller Says Stablecoins to Broaden Reach of US Policy
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller the spread of stablecoins around the world could amplify the influence of US central-bank policy.

item.title: ECB Should Act Sooner Rather Than Later, Pereira Tells Negócios
item.description: The European Central Bank shouldn’t hesitate to act, given elevated consumer-price pressures, according to Governing Council member Alvaro Santos Pereira.

item.title: AI Bubble Debate Gets Real as Chip Stocks Rally Turns Historic
item.description: Chipmakers are by far the hottest stocks in the market, but their recent surge is lending urgency to the debate over whether investors are buying into an artificial-intelligence bubble that’s due to burst.

item.title: Congo Adds Lithium to Strategic Minerals in Higher Tax Bracket
item.description: Democratic Republic of Congo’s lithium miners could soon be required to triple royalty payments after the government approved the inclusion of several metals to its list of strategic minerals.

item.title: EM’s Record Rally Leads Two Hedge Funds to Turn Away Investors
item.description: There’s so much cash going to emerging markets that a pair of hedge funds that specialize in hard-to-reach debt are starting to turn away investors.

item.title: Indonesia Advances Export Control Plan Despite Uncertainty
item.description: Indonesia is pushing ahead with plans to centralize exports of key commodities starting June 1, casting a cloud of regulatory uncertainty over natural resource producers.

item.title: Singapore’s CapitaLand Sheds 10% of China Staff Amid Downturn
item.description: CapitaLand Investment Ltd. said its workforce in China plunged by about 10%, or 365 people, last year as the asset manager continues to grapple with a major real estate crisis in Asia’s largest economy.
